# wellplayed_tournament_step_preset (Resource)

Applies a preset script to a tournament step, producing the step's rule set, and optionally validates the result. Backed by the `applyPresetScript` mutation, the `stepRuleSet` query, and the `validateStepRuleSet` validation job.

The tournament step must already exist — create it with `wellplayed_tournament_step` and reference its `id`. Each apply re-runs the preset and replaces the step's rule set in place, bumping its `version`. When `validate` is `true`, the apply blocks until the WellPlayed validation job finishes and fails if the resulting rule set is invalid.

There is no API to remove a rule set from a step, so destroying this resource only drops it from Terraform state — the last-applied rule set remains on the step until another preset is applied.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Apply a preset script to a tournament step and validate the resulting rule set.
# The tournament, the step, and the preset script are all managed here so
# Terraform builds them in dependency order.
resource "wellplayed_tournament" "championship" {
  title       = "Summer Championship"
  description = "Invitational 5v5 championship."
}

resource "wellplayed_tournament_step" "playoffs" {
  tournament_id = wellplayed_tournament.championship.id

  name        = "Playoffs"
  description = "Single-elimination playoff bracket."
  order       = 1
  type        = "SINGLE_ELIM"
}

resource "wellplayed_preset_script" "single_elim" {
  name   = "Single Elimination"
  script = <<-LUA
    local best_of = params.best_of or 1
    add_best_of_series_resolution_rule(best_of)
    add_winner_advances_rule()
    add_loser_eliminated_rule()
  LUA

  parameters = [
    {
      name     = "team_count"
      type     = "INT"
      required = true
    },
    {
      name          = "best_of"
      type          = "INT"
      required      = false
      default_value = "1"
    },
  ]
}

# Applies the preset to the step and blocks the apply until the resulting rule
# set passes validation.
resource "wellplayed_tournament_step_preset" "playoffs" {
  tournament_step_id = wellplayed_tournament_step.playoffs.id
  preset_script_id   = wellplayed_preset_script.single_elim.id

  parameters = jsonencode({
    team_count = 8
    best_of    = 3
  })

  validate = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `preset_script_id` (String) ID of the preset script to apply (see `wellplayed_preset_script`). Changing it re-applies the new preset to the step.
- `tournament_step_id` (String) ID of the tournament step the preset is applied to (see `wellplayed_tournament_step`). Immutable; changing it applies the preset to a different step and forces replacement.

### Optional

- `parameters` (String) Parameter values passed to the preset script, encoded as a JSON object string (e.g. `jsonencode({ team_count = 8 })`). Must satisfy the parameters declared by the preset script.
- `validate` (Boolean) Whether to validate the resulting rule set after applying the preset. When `true`, the apply blocks until the validation job completes and fails the apply if the rule set is invalid. Defaults to `false`.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (String) When the rule set was created (RFC 3339).
- `id` (String) Identifier of the step rule set produced by applying the preset.
- `preset_name` (String) Name of the preset that produced the rule set, as recorded by the server.
- `team_count` (Number) Number of teams the rule set was built for, if determined by the preset.
- `updated_at` (String) When the rule set was last updated (RFC 3339).
- `validated` (Boolean) Whether the current rule set has passed validation.
- `validated_at` (String) When the rule set was last validated (RFC 3339), if ever.
- `version` (Number) Version of the step rule set. Incremented each time the preset is applied.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Step presets are imported by their tournament step id. Only the resulting rule
# set is recovered; set preset_script_id (and parameters) in configuration to
# match, since the API does not echo them back.
terraform import wellplayed_tournament_step_preset.playoffs step_01h...
```
